notes.match_073: South Africa and Canada have met just once before: a friendly in Durban on 20 November 2007, which South Africa won 2-0 (Teko Modise scored both). This Round of 32 clash is their first competitive meeting — and the first World Cup knockout game for both nations.
notes.match_074: Germany and Paraguay have met twice. Their only competitive game was the 2002 World Cup Round of 16, when Germany won 1-0 in Seogwipo (Oliver Neuville, 88`). They also drew 3-3 in a friendly in Cologne in August 2013. Paraguay have never beaten Germany.
notes.match_075: Netherlands lead the head-to-head 2-1 against Morocco. Their only competitive meeting was USA '94 in Orlando — Netherlands 2-1 (Dennis Bergkamp and Bryan Roy; Hassan Nader for Morocco). Ronald Koeman captained the Dutch that day; he coaches them now. Friendlies since: Morocco won 2-1 in Arnhem (1999); Netherlands won 2-1 in Agadir (2017).
notes.match_076: Brazil lead 11-2-1 across 14 meetings with Japan. Their only World Cup clash was Germany 2006, when Brazil won 4-1 in Dortmund (Ronaldo twice, plus Juninho and Gilberto). Japan stunned Brazil 3-2 in a friendly in October 2025 — their first-ever win over the Selecão.
notes.match_077: France lead the series 12-5-6 against Sweden across 23 games. At World Cups they met twice in 1958 (Sweden won both group games on the way to the final) and twice in 1994 qualifying (one win each). Sweden's most recent win came 2-1 in Stockholm in June 2017 during World Cup qualifying.
notes.match_078: Ivory Coast and Norway have never met in men's international football. This Round of 32 tie is their first senior encounter. (The only previous Côte d'Ivoire-Norway fixture was a 3-1 Norway win at the 2015 Women's World Cup.)
notes.match_079: Mexico dominate the rivalry 17-7-4 across 26 meetings with Ecuador. Their only World Cup meeting was Korea/Japan 2002, when Mexico won 2-1 in Miyagi (Jared Borgetti and Gerardo Torrado; Agustín Delgado for Ecuador). This is their first World Cup knockout tie.
notes.match_080: England and DR Congo have never played before. The Leopards reached the Round of 32 for the first time at this World Cup after beating Uzbekistan 3-1 on matchday three. Several Congo players ply their trade in English club football.
notes.match_081: The USA lead 2-1-0 in three friendlies against Bosnia and Herzegovina: a 4-3 win in Sarajevo in August 2013 (Jozy Altidore hat-trick), a 0-0 draw in Carson in January 2018, and a 1-0 win in Carson in December 2021. This is their first competitive meeting.
notes.match_082: Belgium and Senegal have never met in senior international football. Senegal famously beat France 1-0 in the opening game of Korea/Japan 2002; Belgium's best World Cup finish remains fourth place on home soil in 1986.
notes.match_083: Portugal lead 7-2-1 across 10 meetings with Croatia. Their most famous clash was Euro 2016: Portugal won 1-0 after extra time in the Round of 16 in Lens (Ricardo Quaresma). Croatia's sole win came 2-1 in a friendly in Lisbon in June 2024.
notes.match_084: Spain lead 9-3-4 across 16 meetings with Austria. At a World Cup they met once: Austria beat Spain 2-1 in the 1978 group stage in Buenos Aires. Spain's biggest win was 9-0 in a Euro 2000 qualifier(1999). They have not played since a friendly in 2009.
notes.match_085: Switzerland have won both previous meetings with Algeria, both friendlies: 2-1 in Algiers in November 1983 and 2-0 in Geneva in May 1986. Algeria famously won the 1990 Africa Cup of Nations and reached the World Cup Round of 16 in 2014.
notes.match_086: Argentina and Cape Verde have never met before. Cape Verde are the smallest nation ever to reach a World Cup knockout round, having drawn with Spain and Uruguay in the group stage. Argentina beat Uruguay 1-0 in the 1986 World Cup Round of 16 on their way to lifting the trophy.
notes.match_087: Colombia and Ghana have never met at senior level. Colombia reached the quarter-finals in Brazil 2014; Ghana were quarter-finalists in South Africa 2010.
notes.match_088: Australia and Egypt have met twice. Egypt won 3-0 in a friendly in Cairo in November 2010. Before that, they drew 0-0 at the 1987 President's Cup in Seoul — Australia won 4-3 on penalties. This is their first World Cup meeting.
